documentation_accuracy_summary: Accurate. docs/development/api-conventions.md (lines 168-171) now states bare http://, https://, ftp://, mailto:, and tel: URIs are counted only at a word boundary, with explicit examples (hotel:, motel: not counted). This matches the implementation and the tests exactly. No other doc describes per-post link-counting tokens; no further update is required.
test_sufficiency_summary: Sufficient. 16 new tests cover AC2 negatives (hotel:, motel:, xmailto:, a-tel:, inlined_mailto: -> 0), AC3 positives (mailto:/tel: at start-of-string, after space, newline, tab, '>', '['), AC4 markdown-link single-count (no double-count, no skipPositions regression), and AC5 mixed-body fail-safe (boundary-preceded schemes in a mixed body count correctly; hotel:x and tel:+1 -> 1). All 51 tests verified passing by the verifier: npx pnpm --filter @sfus/api exec vitest run src/common/throttle/link-limit.test.ts returned 51 passed, 0 failed.
